title: 7.0 Coordinate Geometry
description: Grade 10 Math Unit 7 — extremely difficult problems on distance, line division, equations of lines, parallel/perpendicular lines, circles, and applications.

questions.question: Point P(k, 2k) is equidistant from A(2, 0) and B(6, 4). Find the value of k.
The sum of the x-coordinates of all points on the x-axis that are 13 units from (5, -12) is:
A point P(x, y) moves so that its distances from A(1, 3) and B(5, -1) are equal. What is the equation of its locus?
Points A(1, 3), B(5, 11), and C(k, 7) are collinear. Find k.
Points A(-2, 4) and B(4, y) are such that AB = 10. The sum of all possible values of y is:
Point P divides the line segment joining A(1, -2) and B(9, 6) internally in the ratio 3 : 1. Find P.
Point P divides the line segment joining A(2, 1) and B(6, 5) externally in the ratio 2 : 1. Find P.
In what ratio does point P(0, 4) divide the line segment joining A(-2, 3) and B(4, 6) internally?
If M(3, -1) is the midpoint of AB and A has coordinates (7, -5), find the coordinates of B.
Points P and Q trisect the line segment joining A(2, 4) and B(11, 13). Find the distance PQ.
The angle of inclination of a line is 135°. What is its slope?
A line has equation 3x - 4y + 12 = 0. Find the sum of its x-intercept and y-intercept.
A line passes through (2, 3) and (6, -1). What is its y-intercept?
Find the equation of the line parallel to 2x + 3y = 6 that passes through the point (5, -2).
Let L be the line through the intersection of x + y = 5 and 2x - y = 4, perpendicular to 3x + y = 2. Find the equation of L.
Convert 5x - 2y + 10 = 0 to slope-intercept form. Identify the slope m and y-intercept b.
The lines 4x + ky - 7 = 0 and kx + 9y + 2 = 0 are parallel. Find the sum of all possible values of k.
The lines (a + 1)x + 2y - 5 = 0 and 3x + (a - 1)y + 4 = 0 are perpendicular. Find a.
Find the perpendicular distance from point P(3, -2) to the line 4x - 3y + 5 = 0.
Find the equation of the line through P(4, 1) that is parallel to 2x + 3y = 6.
The line L has equation x - 2y + 4 = 0. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to L?
Find the center and radius of the circle x² + y² + 6x - 8y - 11 = 0.
A circle has center (2, -1) and passes through (5, 3). Find its equation in general form.
The endpoints of a diameter of a circle are A(-1, 2) and B(5, 6). Find the equation of the circle in general form.
Find the radius of the circle x² + y² - 10x + 6y + 9 = 0.
The line y = 3x + k is tangent to the circle x² + y² = 10. Find k².
Find the area of the triangle with vertices A(1, 1), B(5, 4), and C(2, 7).
Points A(-3, 2), B(1, 6), and C(5, k) are collinear. Find k.
Find the area of the triangle formed by the lines x = 0, y = 0, and 3x + 4y = 12.
A circle has center (3, 2) and passes through (7, 5). Point P(k, 6) lies on this circle. Find the possible values of k.
